ogTitle: Stanwell Tops vs State Mine Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Stanwell Tops and State Mine Gully.
heading: Stanwell Tops vs State Mine Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stanwell Tops, New South Wales and State Mine Gully, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: State Mine Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Stanwell Tops (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stanwell Tops or State Mine Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stanwell Tops has a flood risk score of 44/100, while State Mine Gully scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stanwell Tops scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. State Mine Gully scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stanwell Tops or State Mine Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. State Mine Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
